title: Delak Krennel
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Update|[[The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire]]}}

{{Quote|I've always viewed generosity as a weakness, to be exploited. Ruthlessly exploited.|Delak Krennel|<ref name="Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Mandatory Retirement">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Mandatory Retirement]]''</ref>}}
'''Delak Krennel''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]]. Krennel would eventually [[Imperial Naval Academy/Legends|enroll]] within the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Krennel was an aggressive commander, and was extremely [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]], displaying hatred towards [[Non-huMan|Non-humans]].

Krennel would ascend to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]], and became the [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Reckoning (Imperial II-class)|''Reckoning'']], and was assigned to serve [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] within the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. Krennel hated Thrawn, and disobeyed direct orders and carried out ruthless strikes against the enemy. This led to Thrawn removing his [[Limb/Legends|arm]], which Krennel would replace with a [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthetic arm]]. Krennel would be sent to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to be punished further, but with the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|loss at]] [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], Krennel received none.

Krennel went on to serve [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Director]] [[Ysanne Isard]], who dispatched Krennel to [[Undercover operation on Axxila|eliminate]] former [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Pestage]], after being removed as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. Krennel would [[Death/Legends|kill]] Pestage, and become a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] through the self-styled title "[[Prince-Admiral/Legends|Prince-Admiral]].", ruling the [[Ciutric Hegemony]]. He would financially support Thrawn when he [[8 ABY/Legends|returned]] from the Unknown Regions to [[Thrawn campaign|fight]] the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Krennel would later desire peace and allow refugees to enter his territory. When the New Republic [[Battle of Distna|recovered plans]] that depicted the warlord supposedly [[Superweapon/Legends|building]] a [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]-[[Pulsar Station|weapon]], New Republic forces would deploy to [[Battle of Ciutric|end]] Krennel's fiefdom. Krennel would eventually die in battle aboard the ''Reckoning'', ending his life and his faction.

==Biography==
Krennel was born on [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]]. At a young age, he demonstrated the firm [[Humanocentrism]] as well as the sadism and penchant for brutality that would mark his later career when he helped his father burn down [[Alien/Legends|alien]]-owned farmlands so they could be seized by a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[agro-combine]].<ref name="Isard" />

He began his Imperial service when he joined the [[Imperial Naval Academy/Legends|Prefsbelt IV Naval Academy]].<ref name="Isard" /> His academy record bore several prizes as an intramural unarmed combat champion. In later years, Krennel still kept himself in fighting trim, and his abilities were only enhanced when his right [[Limb/Legends|hand]] and forearm were replaced by a [[Cybernetics/Legends|mechanical prosthetic]], which was composed of metal and glowed from within with [[Color/Legends|red]] light.<ref name="Isard" />

Upon graduation, Krennel was assigned to active service under [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Rufaan Tigellinus]]. Krennel's brutality grated on his refined commanding officer, and Tigellinus reassigned him to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], where he served under the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] known as [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] as a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in command of the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Reckoning (Imperial II-class)|''Reckoning'']]. Krennel resented his position beneath a nonhuman officer, as well as his post at the Unknown Regions, but could not voice his frustrations to Thrawn, so he instead used his rage to escalate his brutal nature on the alien prisoners. Eventually, Krennel's anger towards Thrawn had him decide to use a [[Force pike/Legends|force pike]] against one of Thrawn's artworks, in mockery of his superior's tendency to utilize alien artwork to exploit an alien race's weakness during battle. This resulted in Thrawn ordering one of his Noghri Death Commandos to cut his arm off from the elbow down before he could damage it. Following the incident, Krennel acquired a new respect for both Thrawn and the power of fear, as well as a prosthetic limb.<ref name="Despoilers">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-1|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>

[[File:Delakkrennelvsysanne.jpg|thumb|left|Delak Krennel and [[Ysanne Isard]], each with their own secret agendas]]

In the wake of the Battle of Endor, Krennel was promoted to the rank of [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]].<ref name="Despoilers" /> On the orders of [[Ysanne Isard]],<ref name="XW29">[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 29|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 29]]</ref> [[Director/Legends|director]] of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]],<ref name="Handbook">[[Star Wars Handbook 1: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''Star Wars Handbook'' 1: ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'']]</ref> he took ''Reckoning'' to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Axxila/Legends|Axxila]] in an attempt to [[Undercover operation on Axxila|capture]] an Alliance force led by [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Krennel had his [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] complement fire upon local civilians to draw out the rebels, but was forced to withdraw, empty-handed, by an [[AT3 Directive]] issued by Imperial defector [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]], posing as an [[Spy/Legends|undercover operative]]. At the insistence of [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], Krennel obeyed, at the same time allowing a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] carrying [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] Captain [[Leonia Tavira]] to jump to [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]]. Unbeknownst to the admiral, Pestage had negotiated with both Tavira and the rebels to secure their support in his own defection.<ref name="XW31" /> Pestage fled [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for [[Ciutric IV]] and Krennel, aligned with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Paltr Carvin]] of the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|tribunal]] that took the regent's place and eager to make up for his deception at Axxila, was assigned on a [[Evacuation of Ciutric|retrieval mission]] to Ciutric. While assessing a tactical [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] for the coming operation, the admiral was intercepted by Isard, who negotiated possession of Pestage for political power and the addition of the [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Interdictor''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Binder]]'' to Krennel's own ''Reckoning''.<ref name="XW33">[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 33|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 33]]</ref>

When Pestage escaped the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], he sought asylum with Krennel, unaware of his hatred for the Grand Vizier. Krennel strangled Pestage and seized his holdings in the [[Ciutric Hegemony]], before coldly murdering [[Pestage family|Pestage's entire family]]. Krennel then styled himself as a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], with the title of "[[Prince-Admiral/Legends|Prince-Admiral]]."<ref name="Isard" />

During [[Thrawn campaign|Thrawn's campaign]], Krennel offered monetary support and pretended to pay heed to his former commander's call for unity.<ref name="Isard" /> In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> after the defeat of Thrawn at [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Bilbringi]], Krennel was targeted to be an example to warlords of the New Republic's newly strengthened policy against them.<ref name="Isard" />

With the [[Ysanne Isard (clone)|clone Isard]], Krennel began a plot to sow chaos in the New Republic between human and nonhuman species, waylay freight traffic to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] at [[Liinade III]], and conquer Coruscant. [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] put their plans to a quick end, with the aid of the original Ysanne Isard. [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] forwarded intelligence provided by Isard to the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]], which dispatched a flotilla to Ciutric before Krennel's forces could launch their attack. The flotilla, under the command of Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] crushed Krennel's forces in a [[Thrawn Pincer]] and seized the shield-stripped capital of the Hegemony. Krennel died aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], ''Reckoning'', when a [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launched from either the ''[[Selonian Fire]]'' or ''[[Corusca Fire]]'' pierced the forward viewport, shattering the viewport and sending shards of transparisteel through Krennel's body. When the missile exploded, Krennel and the entire bridge were destroyed.<ref name="Isard" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Why study the art of a species when I know all I need to know to destroy that species?|Delak Krennel|<ref name="XW33" />}}
Krennel was a competent tactician and strategist (as evidenced by his promotion to Imperial Admiral), but he was a brutal man by nature and had little tolerance for the subtler approaches espoused by two of his old superiors, Thrawn and Isard. His reflexive response to any obstacle was to crush it.

He was very sadistic, as well as masochistic, the latter trait being adopted shortly after he lost his arm to Thrawn when trying to damage one of his artworks.<ref name="Despoilers" /> Krennel had no patience for delays or those he deemed incompetent, and he would react with violent outbursts—going so far as to beat an Isard intelligence operative to death for failing him.<ref name="XW34">[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 34|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 34]]</ref>

During his brief tutelage by Isard's clone as ruler of the Ciutric Hegemony, he began to experiment with political propaganda and other means of manipulating opinion in his favor, presenting himself as a sympathetic ruler rather than a tyrant. He started to see the effectiveness of these methods and flattered himself that he was a quick learner and would soon be able to master them without "Isard's" help. His death in battle cut short any promise he might have shown.<ref name="Isard" />
